authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 5182xx: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines the official industry group and service lineage for data centers and cloud service operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry context, terminology, and role boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-145, The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides official definitions and delivery-role boundaries required to avoid generic service-workflow assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cloud deployment scope and shared-responsibility framing

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-146, Cloud Computing Synopsis and Recommendations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines cloud risk and operational recommendation stages, including readiness and governance considerations.
used_for: evidence expectations for safe service handoff

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61r2, Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides incident-response workflow stages used when implementation-support work is service-impacting.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 27017, Cloud Security Controls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines control expectations for cloud services and shared responsibility, directly relevant to evidence and ownership checks in support workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 27018, Personal Data Protection in Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets data-protection handling requirements for customer PII in cloud engagements, constraining what support can and cannot resolve without authority.
used_for: refusal handling for sensitive record requests

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1, Information Technology Service Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides service-management patterns for support request handling, communication, and handoff quality relevant to this lane.

workflow_stages.name: Service request intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect request intent, tenant context, approval ownership, and required evidence before classifying in scope or routing.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Implementation evidence and dependency capture
workflow_stages.description: Validate runbook, change, validation, and rollback artifacts against ticketing, CMDB, and approval systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and source-conflict handling
workflow_stages.description: Compare public and tenant sources for conflicts, prioritize authority safely, and escalate unresolved ambiguity without confident synthesis.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Resolution, communication, and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Emit service resolution when supported, or explicit escalation and handoff artifacts when boundaries, conflicts, or dependencies block completion.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Runbook, change request, and deployment plan

allowed: Classify and route implementation-support requests using tenant-approved evidence and systems-of-record.
Prepare service resolution and escalation artifacts when all required prerequisites are confirmed.
Escalate to adjacent lanes for legal, policy waiver, financial, safety, or engineering authority.
escalate: Missing implementation artifacts or unavailable systems-of-record required to prove readiness.
Conflicts between public guidance and tenant policy that change risk posture.
Request would require discretionary exception, refund, or compensation decision.
Source ambiguity that materially changes support classification.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, policy waiver, pricing/compensation, or engineering authority.
Completion or handoff without required evidence and source confirmation.
Discretionary shortcuts that bypass approval, validation, or security gates.

domain_failure_modes: Marking a cloud request as supported without validating implementation artifacts and rollback evidence.
Overgeneralizing by applying generic support playbooks and ignoring cloud-specific terminology such as migration wave, tenancy partition, and runbook stage.
Routing to wrong owner when billing/legal/compliance authority boundaries are triggered.
Escalating too late when ticketing/CMDB systems are unavailable or contradictory.
Ignoring privacy-sensitive customer data constraints and proceeding without explicit authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant classification of safety-critical implementation classes
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant risk tags and engineering-safe boundaries must be supplied in runtime context before any escalation-to-closeout action.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional privacy and data residency obligations
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Regional policy overlays are required runtime inputs and are treated as mandatory escalation conditions where applicable.
Lane never owns refund/compensation approval; always routes with evidence and unresolved-item context.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because material differences are converted into explicit tenant-required retrieval or escalation conditions.
Authoritative sources span cloud-specific terminology, operation workflows, and control expectations with explicit freshness and decay posture.
Scenario set enforces policy exceptions, missing facts, boundary routing, repeat failures, conflict handling, and communication handoffs.
